summary: Full backend + frontend testing of SafeRoute Chennai. All 17 pytest backend tests passed. All key frontend flows (home search, autocomplete, route analysis, breakdown modal, journey, Walk-With-Me, SOS modal, Watch page with SOS banner, Report screen, Dashboard) working with real Chennai data.

backend_endpoint_results.GET /api/geocode?q=T+Nagar: 200 - real Nominatim results in Chennai bbox
backend_endpoint_results.POST /api/routes (T Nagar->Egmore, walki: 200 - 2 routes, safety score 90, 6-factor breakdown, sorted safest-first, weights sum=1.0
backend_endpoint_results.GET /api/dashboard/stats: 200 - verified=11, safe_places=3011, police=64, hospitals=1258, metros=45, data_sources present
backend_endpoint_results.WebSocket /api/ws/journeys/{token}: Connected, received location broadcast after REST ping

frontend_flow_results.Home /: Loads, brand-pill/dashboard-link/mode selectors/analyze button all present
frontend_flow_results.Autocomplete search-source (T Nagar): Debounced, Nominatim results shown; selection populates input
frontend_flow_results.Analyze -> route cards: 2 route cards rendered (Safest score 90, Alternative score 90) with ETA/km/verified/landmarks/confidence, route lines shown on map
frontend_flow_results.Why this score modal: Opens with all 6 factors (Incident History, Lighting, Nearby Safe Places, Community Confidence, Time of Day, Route Complexity), weight/score/confidence/detail/source visible
frontend_flow_results.Start journey: Navigates to /journey/{token}, journey-back, walk-with-me-btn, end-journey-btn, sos-button all present
frontend_flow_results.Walk-With-Me modal: Opens with share URL (https://.../watch/{token}), copy-link-btn, whatsapp-btn all present
frontend_flow_results.SOS modal: Opens with call-100, call-1091, call-108, confirm-sos-btn buttons
frontend_flow_results./watch/{token}: Loads with destination label 'Egmore', safety score badge (78 High), Live tracking indicator, 100/1091/108 emergency cards (data-testid watch-emergency-100/1091/108), and shows sos-banner 'EMERGENCY SOS ACTIVE' when sos triggered
frontend_flow_results./report: Loads with 6 category tiles (harassment/stalking/theft/poor_lighting/suspicious/other), description-input, use-my-gps, submit-report-btn
frontend_flow_results./dashboard: Shows verified_incidents=11, safe_places_mapped=3011, police_stations=64, hospitals=1258, metro_stations=45, scoring breakdown, and data sources list

action_items: Minor: review request expects testid 'watch-call-100' on Watch screen but code uses 'watch-emergency-100/1091/108'. Both call-testids are functionally present; consider adding a 'watch-call-100' alias if strict adherence to spec is required.

Minor: /report screen does not render a nearby-reports list (no nearby-incident-* / confirm-* / dispute-* testids found). The review request lists Confirm/Dispute buttons and nearby reports list — this UI is missing on ReportScreen. Backend supports it via /api/incidents?lat&lng and /api/incidents/{id}/confirm.
Minor: SOS modal is dismissed by clicking backdrop or Cancel button, but pressing Escape does not close it — small UX enhancement.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 1044-line file. Consider splitting into modules (routes.py, journeys.py, incidents.py, safety_scoring.py, seed.py) for maintainability.

In /api/routes label logic, 'Fastest' can be assigned to the same route as 'Safest' if it happens to also be the shortest — the else branch handles this correctly, but consider marking dual labels ('Safest & Fastest') for clarity.
score_route() uses in-memory Python bbox scan against safe_places. With 3011 POIs this is fine, but for scale consider MongoDB 2dsphere geospatial index and $near queries.

The community_score falls back to 75 with confidence 0.3 when no incidents nearby — good honest behavior. Consider surfacing this transparently in the breakdown detail text (currently just says '0 community-verified concerns').
Incident rate-limit check compares ISO-string timestamp to string via $gte — this happens to work for ISO-8601 but is fragile. Use datetime objects with proper Mongo BSON dates.
No auth is by design (privacy-first), but /api/incidents/{id}/confirm has no rate limit — a single client could inflate verified_count. Consider IP/session-based rate-limit.
MissingObjectId serialization: incidents/safe_places endpoints correctly exclude _id and expose 'id' string. Good.
ReportScreen missing nearby-incidents UI mentioned in spec — see action item.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all core flows working; minor testid + missing nearby-reports list on /report)

test_credentials: N/A - app is anonymous, no auth
seed_data_creation: Created a few TEST_pytest incidents and journeys via API tests. Journeys auto-expire in 6h; incidents remain but are marked source=community.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_saferoute_api.py — all 17 pass. Frontend key testids verified: brand-pill, dashboard-link, search-source[-locate][-result-N], search-destination[-result-N], mode-{walking,cycling,driving}, find-routes-btn, route-card-N, route-why-N, start-journey-N, breakdown-modal, close-breakdown, report-incident-btn, journey-back, walk-with-me-btn, end-journey-btn, sos-button, share-modal, share-url, copy-link-btn, whatsapp-btn, sos-modal, call-100, call-1091, call-108, confirm-sos-btn, sos-banner, watch-emergency-100/1091/108, report-back, category-*, description-input, use-my-gps, submit-report-btn, dashboard-back, stats-grid. Missing testids from spec: watch-call-100 (only watch-emergency-* exist), nearby-incident-*/confirm-*/dispute-* on report screen (feature not implemented in UI).
